mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation forces monetary authorities to tighten policy aggressively, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint high readings in retail sales and CPI fail to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which boosts near-term corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a rolling correlation coefficient below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained housing starts above the 1500 threshold signal robust construction sector demand and rising household wealth effects, which stimulate consumer spending and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts above 1500 fail to precede positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market tightening, which supports aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields a negative or zero average forward return over 60-day windows in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp federal funds rate hike during an inflationary regime abruptly raises borrowing costs and discount rates, triggering immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25pp in a single meeting
failure_condition: Rate hikes of 0.25pp or more fail to produce negative 5-day returns in >65% of observed instances during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue streams, which systematically supports higher equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a win rate exceeding 50% across multiple business cycles.
